ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term specific to Turkish ekmek exists; closest_parent 'bread food product' (FOODON_03301710) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000E (Bread and rolls) is the closest defensible parent; no ekmek-specific FoodEx2 term exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 'Bread (food)' is the closest parent; no Turkish ekmek-specific SNOMED CT code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of plain ekmek is one medium slice or small roll weighing approximately 80 g, as commonly served alongside Middle Eastern or Turkish meals.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 18069)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Plain ekmek is a Turkish white wheat bread with a composition closely analogous to USDA SR Legacy white bread (NDB 18069 / FDC 168012). Values adapted from this entry. Turkish ekmek is a simple yeast-leavened white bread; no significant compositional deviation from standard white bread is expected. Sodium adjusted slightly downward to reflect typical Turkish ekmek recipes which use moderate salt.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — white bread, Turkish-type
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 71 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI table published in the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, which lists white wheat bread (Turkish-style) with a GI of approximately 71 (glucose reference = 100). Glycemic load per 80 g serving calculated as: (71 × 49.0 × 0.80) / 100 = 27.7.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of plain ekmek to one small slice (40–50 g) per meal, as a single 80 g serving contributes approximately 392 mg sodium, representing roughly 17% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by major cardiovascular guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 71) and high refined carbohydrate content (~49 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain ekmek is a high-GI refined white bread that can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict portions to half a small roll (approximately 40 g) and pair it with protein, fat, or fibre-rich foods to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate and moderate sodium; low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain ekmek is low in saturated fat (0.7 g per 100 g), which is favourable for cardiovascular health, but its high refined carbohydrate content and moderate sodium level warrant portion control for individuals with cardiovascular disease, particularly those managing dyslipidaemia or hypertension concurrently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain ekmek is the traditional Turkish staple bread, a simple yeast-leavened white wheat bread baked in a conventional oven. The word 'ekmek' means 'bread' in Turkish and refers broadly to the everyday loaf consumed throughout Turkey and the wider Middle Eastern and Eastern Mediterranean region. It is made from refined wheat flour, water, yeast, and salt, producing a soft, slightly chewy crumb with a thin golden crust. The texture is light and airy, with a mild, slightly yeasty flavour. In Singapore, plain ekmek is served at Middle Eastern restaurants such as Sahara Bar & Restaurant as an accompaniment to mezze, grilled meats, shawarma, and kebabs. Nutritionally, plain ekmek is a high-carbohydrate, moderate-protein, low-fat food with a high glycemic index (GI ~71), making it a significant source of rapidly available glucose. It provides useful amounts of B vitamins (thiamine, niacin, folate) due to flour enrichment, and moderate iron. Its sodium content (~490 mg per 100 g) is relevant for individuals managing hypertension. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume it in small portions paired with protein or fibre to moderate postprandial glycaemia. The bread is naturally vegan, halal, and dairy-free, but contains gluten and is unsuitable for those with coeliac disease or wheat allergy.
common_user_queries: What is the sodium content of ekmek?
What bread is served with shawarma?
meal_context: bread basket at Middle Eastern restaurants
dipping bread for hummus or dips

confidence_reason: No Singapore-specific or Turkish-specific approved primary source (HPB, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for plain ekmek. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy white bread (FDC 168012), which is the closest defensible approved-source analogue. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI table for white wheat bread (Turkish-type). Confidence is medium because the match is adapted rather than exact.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for white bread (FDC 168012 / NDB 18069), which is the closest approved-source analogue for plain ekmek. No direct ekmek entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source for this bread type; no industrial trans fats are present in standard white bread. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as plain ekmek contains no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; white bread contains negligible vitamin C. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; unenriched white bread contains no significant vitamin A. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; no animal products present. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; white bread is not a source of vitamin D. 3. Sodium value (490 mg per 100 g) is adapted from USDA SR Legacy white bread; actual sodium in restaurant-prepared ekmek may vary. 4. GI of 71 is from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008) Am J Clin Nutr, for white wheat bread (Turkish-type). Glycemic load per 80 g serving = (71 × 49.0 × 0.80) / 100 = 27.7. 5. Ontology: No FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT term specific to Turkish ekmek exists; closest_parent codes used throughout. 6. Multilingual names: Plain ekmek is a Turkish/Middle Eastern food; it is not a Singaporean dish, so Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi mandatory names do not apply under G6. The romanized Turkish name 'Ekmek' and 'Beyaz ekmek' (white bread in Turkish) are captured in aliases. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 not used; value 0.6 from USDA source). polyunsaturated_fat_g value 1.4 from USDA source. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g fields are not present in this record as USDA SR Legacy provides comprehensive micronutrient data for white bread.
